_note: CITYLIFE — the PROBE RIG for TEMPLATE #2 (person + schedule). Blocks don't reveal under the plain `live` driver (nothing dives), so this is a SLICE of Atlas that IS revealed: the same city CLOCK, one active block, a handful of people who live a DAY. The person rules/actions/events here are the EXACT text destined for Atlas's block generator — only the spawn wiring + broadcast-chain length differ (world structure, not the template). THE GATE (read the action tally): choices must SPREAD across sleep/work/eat/socialize by time of day. If one action dominates ~100%, the schedule is broken. `mean food` (= 1-hunger) should ripple as people get hungry and eat.

generators._block_note: cascade:true reveals the block (Detailed) so its people are ACTIVE — is_active(person) needs every ancestor Detailed (city is reveal:true, block is revealed here).

generators._venue_note: ★ VENUES (TEMPLATE #3 + #4 woven in) — two DELIBERATELY IDENTICAL venues (same hours, same base appeal) so NOTHING authored favours either. Which one wins a given night emerges from a WEB of independent signals, none of which writes the outcome: a hidden nightly FOOD_QUALITY roll (first guests gamble), a STOCK of mains that depletes as the place gets busy (success breeds scarcity), WORD-of-mouth (mean fun of who's there now — later guests get informed), personal FUN (the food you got × the company), and the crowd pull. A popular venue with bad food tonight fills first, then empties as word spreads / it runs out — and the underdog wins. Nobody scripts that.

broadcasts._clock_down_note: Push the city CLOCK down to the block so the person's action scores can read parent.is_night / parent.is_work. One level here (block is a direct child of city); in Atlas it's two levels (city→district→block).

broadcasts._hour_to_venues_note: relay hour one more level, block→children, so venues can gate their own open-hours.

rules._needs_note: ★ NEEDS DECAY (template #2) — energy drains while awake, hunger climbs, social fades. The actions below replenish them; the balance of decay-vs-replenish is what makes a routine EMERGE rather than being scripted. `food` = 1-hunger, purely so the live driver's readout ripples.

rules._fun_note: ★ FUN (the personal read of the night) — while you're at a venue (guest edge live), your fun = the food you were actually SERVED (0.7) plus the buzz of the COMPANY (0.3, from the venue's crowd, both read via edge_mean over the guest edge). Off the edge it decays. This is what you FEEL; it feeds the venue's word (others learn from it) AND your own stay-or-go — a bad plate or an empty room lowers fun and you drift off.

rules._venue_rules_note: ★ VENUE STATE + THE EMERGENT WEB (templates #3/#4). Author order = evaluation order, so each reads fresh values. is_open from broadcast hour. crowd = guests linked now (bidirectional edge_count). served = the quality actually on the plate: the hidden food_quality WHILE stock lasts, then a poor 0.15 once the mains run out (scarcity bites). word = mean FUN of who's here now (edge_mean over the guest edge) — this is information: it only reflects reality once people have been in and formed an opinion, so early arrivals can't read it. appeal_now (what the @max pick ranks) combines THREE independent pulls — the crowd (herd), the word (quality info), and noise — none of which alone names a winner.

rules._appeal_note: appeal_now = a standing draw (the venue's own `appeal` — the popular spot vs the dive, so business is realistically UNEVEN, never 50/50) + word-of-mouth + a WORD-GATED crowd pull (0.15*crowd*word: a crowd only attracts while it's having a GOOD TIME, so a burnt-out room stops pulling and people flee → migration) + a noise floor OUTSIDE is_open (so the opening isn't a 0-vs-0 tie that the id order would break the same way every night). The dominant venue usually wins — but a great food roll at the dive, or the favourite running dry, flips it. That's real: a spot that's usually packed, occasionally upset.

actions._schedule_note: ★ THE SCHEDULE (template #2) — utility AI gated on the CLOCK, NOT on scheduled-at-time events (the ⚠ untested prim). Continuous clock-gates (parent.is_night, parent.is_work) sidestep it entirely. Highest-scoring action wins each tick, so the routine is emergent: sleep pulls hard at night (harder when tired), work pulls in work hours, eat spikes with hunger any time, socialising fills the evenings/gaps. `at`: 0 home, 1 work, 2 out, 3 eatery — the location the pawn-render pass will read.
actions.name: sleep

actions._work_tune_note: SOFT energy term (0.4 + 0.5*energy) instead of a hard gt(energy,0.15) gate — a tired worker slows but doesn't drop dead to idle; the `idle`/break action (which SPIKES when energy<0.3) pulls them off for a breather, then work reclaims. This work→tire→break→work loop is why work is the daytime plurality without anyone running on empty.
actions.name: work

actions._headout_note: ★ HEAD OUT (template #3) — pick a venue ONCE (gated on lt(edge_count(guest),1), so no per-tick re-pick → no sloshing). `link guest:venue@max:target.appeal_now` grabs the highest-appeal open venue; the busy-feedback makes newcomers join whoever's winning. The 0.55 BASE (not just a social-need term) matters for MIGRATION: after a venue burns out and you leave unsatisfied, you STILL want to be out — so you re-head_out and, since the dry venue's appeal has tanked, land next door. That's the crowd moving, unscripted.
actions.name: head_out

actions._stayout_note: ★ STAY OUT — commitment (kills the sloshing) AND the exit valve. Score rises with how much FUN you're having, so a great night holds you and a bad one (weak food / ran out of mains / empty room → low fun) lets sleep or a break outbid this → you unlink and drift off, maybe to re-pick the OTHER venue next: that's MIGRATION, never scripted. `affect guest:stock -0.14` = you eat — every guest draws the venue's mains down, so the busiest venue burns through stock fastest (success breeds its own scarcity).
actions.name: stay_out

actions._break_note: the breather — a low floor (0.25) most of the time, but SPIKES to ~0.85 when energy<0.3 so an exhausted person rests instead of grinding. Recovers energy faster than the drain, so it's a real recovery, not a stall.
actions.name: idle

events._clock_events_note: day rhythm on the always-active city (template #1).
events.label: 🌅 a new day breaks over Veranholm

events._person_events_note: colour for the chronicle — rising-edge per person, re-arms when the need recovers.
events.label: is running on empty
goes hungry, nothing in the cupboard

events._gathering_note: ★ THE GATHERING (template #3) — rising-edge when a venue's crowd crosses a threshold: a scene kicks off. `affect guest:social` pushes a warmth bump onto every linked guest. Re-arms when the crowd thins, so it fires each time a venue fills.
events.label: 🎉 the place fills up — a scene kicks off

events._open_roll_note: ★ THE NIGHTLY ROLL (independent signal, unscripted) — at opening each night the kitchen's form is a fresh dice roll (food_quality) and the mains are restocked. rand at the open tick differs every night, so tonight's quality is NOT correlated with last night's or with the other venue's — the thing that makes each night its own story. Tight band so it fires once at open; re-arms when the venue closes.
events.label: 🍳 opens for the night

events._stockout_note: the mains run out — a consequence of being TOO popular (crowd drained the stock). served drops to scraps, fun craters, the crowd starts to leave. Pure feedback, no schedule.
events.label: 🍽 runs out of the main course
🌟 word gets around — the spot tonight
😐 the room sours — the food's off tonight
